baseline_note: Every feature benchmark implicitly expects Domain (model + repository protocol), Data (repository impl), and Presentation (ViewModel @Observable @MainActor + SwiftUI View). Expectations below test only what is unique to each scenario.

evals.prompt: I want to build a fitness app called FitTracker where users can create profiles and log their workouts
evals.expected_output: Full new-app scaffold: Tuist project config, app entry point, root navigation, and first feature with all three layers
expectations: Generates Tuist project configuration files
Generates an app entry point (e.g. @main App struct with WindowGroup)
Generates root navigation (NavigationStack or TabView)
First feature includes all three layers (Domain/Data/Presentation) with compile-ready Swift files

evals.prompt: Help me start a recipe app from scratch — I need categories, a search bar, and a way to save favorites
evals.expected_output: Multi-feature app scaffold where each feature owns its models and repository locally
expectations: Generates at least two distinct feature modules (e.g. Categories, Favorites)
Each feature owns its own models and repository (feature-local, no shared bucket)
No catch-all Shared/Models or Shared/Data directories

evals.prompt: I want to add a Notes feature that saves everything locally on the device so it works offline
evals.expected_output: Feature with GRDB-backed repository, .task modifier for loading, and protocol-driven DI
expectations: Implements repository with GRDB read/write methods
View includes .task modifier to trigger initial data load
ViewModel uses protocol-driven repository injection via init parameter

evals.prompt: I need a Weather screen that pulls data from an API — no need to save anything locally
evals.expected_output: API-only feature with no database code — repository uses URLSession or network client
expectations: Repository implementation uses URLSession or network client instead of local database
No GRDB imports, database records, or migration code generated

evals.prompt: I want to add analytics tracking that works across all features in my app — every screen should be able to log events
evals.expected_output: Cross-domain shared service with domain protocol, treated as exception to feature-local default
expectations: Treats shared module as exception, not default
Defines a domain protocol for the service
Service is domain-scoped within Shared (e.g. Shared/Analytics) with its own layer split

evals.prompt: Add a Notifications feature to my app that fetches notification history from the API and caches it so it works without internet
evals.expected_output: Feature with Domain/Data/Presentation layers, API repository, and local cache or GRDB storage for offline access
expectations: Creates a notification domain model and repository protocol
Implements a repository that fetches from API and caches or persists results locally
Generates a ViewModel (@Observable @MainActor) and SwiftUI view
No GRDB types leak into the Domain layer

evals.prompt: I want to pull my networking code into its own local Swift Package so other modules can reuse it
evals.expected_output: Local SPM package with separate interface and implementation targets
expectations: Generates a Package.swift with at least two targets (interface + implementation)
Separates domain protocols from concrete implementation

evals.prompt: My UserProfile code is a mess — everything is in one file. Can you split it into proper layers?
evals.expected_output: Refactors monolith into Domain/Data/Presentation layers with feature-local ownership
expectations: Splits existing code into at least three separate layer files or directories
Keeps models and repository local to the feature (no promotion to Shared)
